market_sentiment.label: 中性
market_sentiment.summary: 資料更新中，請稍後再試

articles.title: Joe Lubin-backed Ethereum treasury firm SharpLink to join the Russel  indexes
articles.description: The inclusion could attract fresh passive inflows from index-tracking funds, while the firm's stock tanked 95% from its peak over the past year.

articles.title: SpaceX’s Starlink nabs American Airlines contract, another win for its IPO
articles.description: American Airlines said Tuesday it plans to install Starlink on more than 500 Airbus aircraft, the latest carrier win for IPO-bound SpaceX.

articles.title: UK authorities sanction HTX crypto exchange over support for Russia
articles.description: Formerly Huobi Global, the exchange is the latest entity to be named as part of a crackdown on companies “exploited by Russia to circumvent UK sanctions.”

articles.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
articles.description: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

articles.title: TeraWulf acquires Kentucky AI data center site with planned 1 GW capacity
articles.description: WULF shares surged on Tuesday after the Bitcoin miner announced its latest move to expand into AI and HPC through a multi-phase buildout through 2030.

articles.title: UK sanctions Huobi and ruble stablecoin issuer in crackdown on Russia crypto networks
articles.description: Britain applied banking-style sanctions to crypto exchanges for the first time, requiring U.K. financial firms to freeze funds and trace transactions.

articles.title: Spanish authorities block Polymarket and Kalshi over gambling laws
articles.description: Spain joined a growing list of national authorities restricting or banning prediction market platforms amid concerns over unlicensed gambling activities.

articles.title: Bitcoin 'liquidation hunts' control BTC at $77K amid new Iran peace deal doubts
articles.description: Bitcoin failed to copy US stocks' upside after a spike to $78,000 became one of a series of liquidity grabs.

articles.title: NEAR token price has 'potential to grow 20x,' says Arthur Hayes
articles.description: NEAR is mirroring a 2023–2024 fractal that preceded a 250% rally, hinting at similar upside in 2026 as privacy coin demand grows.

articles.title: Bermuda, the small island nation with huge crypto ambitions
articles.description: The government recently announced it would become the world’s first economy to go fully onchain, a move officials believe will bring its people new opportunities.

articles.title: Coinbase’s Base launches AI tool for ChatGPT to manage crypto wallets and DeFi apps
articles.description: The product, called Base MCP, connects a user’s Base Account to AI clients such as ChatGPT, Claude and Cursor using the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an emerging standard that allows AI systems to securely interface with external tools and applications.

articles.title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
articles.description: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training data that AI and robotics labs are racing to acquire.

articles.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
articles.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

articles.title: XRPL could close its biggest DeFi gap if new AMM amendment passes
articles.description: A draft proposal filed Tuesday would extend the XRP Ledger's native automated market maker with three swappable curve types, giving liquidity providers more efficient ways to deploy capital and closing one of XRPL DeFi's longest-standing gaps.

articles.title: Ghost hackers: the cybersecurity mystery that nobody has solved
articles.description: A shadowy group that stole and dumped the NSA’s most powerful hacking tools still has implications for how companies think about digital risk today.

articles.title: Iranian hackers blamed for breach of Los Angeles transit system that took weeks to recover
articles.description: An Israeli cybersecurity firm said Iran’s government is behind Ababil of Minab, a fake hacktivist persona that has claimed a series of data breaches after the start of the war in Iran.

articles.title: AI guardrail removals raise questions over limits of open-source model regulation
articles.description: Financial Times testing found safety controls on open AI models from Meta and Google could be stripped in minutes, raising governance concerns.

articles.title: The Clarity Act won’t lead to adoption without crypto tax reform
articles.description: The legislation is being framed as a turning point for U.S. crypto policy, but there’s a major piece of the puzzle being overlooked, argues Singh.

articles.title: Ferrari’s first EV is not for you
articles.description: The Ferrari Luce seems to be more aimed at regulatory compliance and China, putting a lot of pressure on the Jony Ive-designed EV.

articles.title: Strategy buys back $1.5B of debt at discount, cuts outstanding notes to $6.7B
articles.description: Strategy reduced aggregate convertible notes outstanding to $6.7 billion after repurchasing 2029 notes for $1.38 billion in cash.
